quiz.title: Unit 11 Quiz 8, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Br√łnsted-Lowry Theory and Conjugate Pairs
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Br√łnsted-Lowry Theory, Conjugate Acid-Base Pairs, Amphoteric Substances, Proton Transfer Reactions

questions.question: Which is a conjugate acid‚Äďbase pair?
questions.explanation: HCl and Cl‚ĀĽ differ by one proton (H‚Āļ). When HCl donates a proton, it becomes Cl‚ĀĽ, making them a conjugate acid-base pair.

questions.question: Which substance can act as both an acid and a base?
questions.explanation: Water (H‚āāO) is amphoteric ‚ÄĒ it can act as both an acid (donating a proton to become OH‚ĀĽ) and a base (accepting a proton to become H‚āÉO‚Āļ).

questions.question: A substance that can act as both an acid and a base is called:
questions.explanation: An amphoteric substance can act as both an acid and a base. Water is the most common example, but others include Al(OH)‚āÉ and many metal hydroxides.

questions.question: In the reaction NH‚āÉ + H‚āāO ‚Üí NH‚āĄ‚Āļ + OH‚ĀĽ, what is the Br√łnsted‚ÄďLowry acid?
questions.explanation: H‚āāO donates a proton to NH‚āÉ, forming OH‚ĀĽ and NH‚āĄ‚Āļ. Since H‚āāO donates a proton, it acts as the Br√łnsted-Lowry acid.

questions.question: In the reaction NH‚āÉ + H‚āāO ‚Üí NH‚āĄ‚Āļ + OH‚ĀĽ, what is the conjugate acid?
questions.explanation: NH‚āÉ accepts a proton to become NH‚āĄ‚Āļ. Therefore, NH‚āĄ‚Āļ is the conjugate acid of the base NH‚āÉ.

questions.question: Which species is the strongest Br√łnsted‚ÄďLowry acid?
questions.explanation: H‚āÉO‚Āļ (hydronium ion) is the strongest Br√łnsted-Lowry acid among these options. It readily donates protons to bases. NH‚āÉ is actually a base, OH‚ĀĽ is a strong base, and H‚āāO is neutral with weak acidic properties.

questions.question: Which theory explains acid‚Äďbase reactions in non-aqueous solutions?
questions.explanation: Br√łnsted-Lowry theory defines acids as proton donors and bases as proton acceptors, which applies to all solvents and even gas-phase reactions, unlike Arrhenius theory which is limited to aqueous solutions.

questions.question: In the reaction H‚āāSO‚āĄ + H‚āāO ‚Üí H‚āÉO‚Āļ + HSO‚āĄ‚ĀĽ, what is the conjugate base?
questions.explanation: H‚āāSO‚āĄ donates a proton to become HSO‚āĄ‚ĀĽ. Therefore, HSO‚āĄ‚ĀĽ is the conjugate base of the acid H‚āāSO‚āĄ.

questions.question: What happens to an acid after it donates a proton? It becomes a/its _____________
questions.explanation: When an acid donates a proton, it becomes its conjugate base. For example, HCl (acid) donates a proton to become Cl‚ĀĽ (conjugate base).

questions.question: Which pair represents a conjugate acid‚Äďbase pair?
questions.explanation: NH‚āĄ‚Āļ and NH‚āÉ differ by one proton. NH‚āĄ‚Āļ can donate a proton to become NH‚āÉ, making them a conjugate acid-base pair.
